question: What is the theme of Nelson Mandela International Day 2026?
options: It Is in Your Hands to Build Peace
It's Still in Our Hands to Combat Poverty and Inequity
detail:
- The theme of **Nelson Mandela International Day 2026** is **"It's Still in Our Hands to Combat Poverty and Inequity"**.
- The day is observed every year on **18 July**, the birth anniversary of **Nelson Mandela**.
- Its purpose is to promote the values of **Peace, Human Rights, Equality, Reconciliation and Social Justice**.
- The **United Nations General Assembly (UNGA)** declared 18 July as Nelson Mandela International Day in **November 2009**, and the first observance was held on **18 July 2010**.
- **About Nelson Mandela:** he was the **first President of South Africa** and its **first black President**, known for his long struggle against **Apartheid**.
- He was awarded the **Nobel Peace Prize in 1993** and the **Bharat Ratna in 1990**.
- He was the **second foreign citizen** to receive the Bharat Ratna — the first was **Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan**, who was given the award in **1987**.

question: Which became the first Indian state to enact the 'Women Farmers Empowerment Act, 2026', granting legal recognition to women farmers?
detail:
- **Maharashtra** has become the **first state in India** to give women legal status as **Farmers**.
- For this the state government enacted the **Maharashtra Women Farmers Empowerment Act, 2026**.
- The law aims to ensure women equal access to agriculture-related **Rights, Government Schemes and Financial Facilities**.
- Under the Act a **Woman Farmer Certificate (WFC)** will be issued, so that a woman farmer can take the benefit of government schemes **even without ownership of a Land Title**.
- A **Centralised Digital Registry** will be created under the law to keep the record of certified women farmers.
- The law also includes women engaged in **Crop Farming, Animal Husbandry, Poultry, Fishery, Agroforestry, Vermiculture, Mushroom Cultivation and Primary Processing** in the category of farmer.
- **Key Point:** the law was brought in during the **International Year of the Woman Farmer** being observed by the **UN**.

question: The 'Korku' tribe in the news mainly resides in which state of India?
detail:
- The **Korku Community of Madhya Pradesh** protested against the action to remove encroachment from forest land.
- They say they have been living and farming in the **Satpura forests** for generations, so they should be given their **Traditional Forest Rights** under the **Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006** and should not be evicted.
- In **July 2026** thousands of Korku people gathered at the **Khandwa district headquarters**; about **2,000 Korku tribals** from roughly **150 villages** of **Khandwa and Burhanpur** took part.
- **About the Korku:** they are a **Scheduled Tribe (ST)** of **Madhya Pradesh**, considered to belong to the **Munda (Kolarian)** language family. The word **'Korku' means "Human Group"**.
- They live mainly in **Madhya Pradesh** and the **Melghat** region of **Maharashtra**, chiefly in the **Khandwa, Burhanpur, Betul and Chhindwara** districts.
- Their livelihood depends mainly on **Agriculture** and **Minor Forest Produce** such as **Mahua, Chironji, Tendu Leaves** and medicinal plants.
- **About the FRA, 2006:** its objective is to give **Scheduled Tribes** and other traditional forest dwellers their traditional rights over forests. It recognises the rights only of those who have been traditionally living on forest land **on or before 13 December 2005** and who fulfil the eligibility conditions.

question: According to the Brand Finance India 100 Report 2026, which brand has retained its position as India's Most Valuable Brand for the 10th consecutive year?
detail:
- **Brand Finance** released the **Brand Finance India 100 Report 2026**, in which the **Tata Group** remained India's **Most Valuable Brand for the 10th consecutive year**.
- The **Adani Group** jumped from **13th to 8th** position, becoming the **fastest entrant into the Top 10**.
- The purpose of the report is to assess the **Brand Value** and **Brand Strength** of Indian companies.
- **Top Highlights:** the total brand value of the **Top 100 Brands** was **USD 252.8 Billion**, which is **7% more** than the previous year.
- **Infosys (USD 16.4 billion)** and **LIC Group (USD 15.3 billion)** stood **second** and **third** respectively.
- **Suzlon Energy** became the **Fastest-growing Indian Brand of 2026** with **114% growth**.
- **Taj Hotels** remained India's **Strongest Brand for the 5th consecutive year**.
- **About Brand Finance:** an independent **Brand Valuation Consultancy** that assesses brand value and brand strength and releases annual rankings. Headquarters **London, United Kingdom**; founded **1996**; Founder & Chairman **David Haigh**.
- **About the Tata Group:** India's largest multinational business conglomerate, working in **Automobile, IT, Steel, Power, Hospitality, Telecom and Consumer Products**. Founded **1868**; Founder **Jamsetji Tata**; headquarters **Mumbai, Maharashtra**; Chairman **N. Chandrasekaran**.

question: Two new species of bees have been discovered in the Siang Valley. Siang Valley is located in which state?
detail:
- Researchers of **ATREE (Ashoka Trust for Research in Ecology and the Environment)** discovered two new rare **Solitary Bee** species in the **Siang Valley of Arunachal Pradesh**.
- The two species are **Elaphropoda triangulata** and **Habropoda adi**.
- **Elaphropoda triangulata** was named after the **triangular abdominal markings** on its body.
- **Habropoda adi** was named in honour of the **Adi tribe** of Arunachal Pradesh.
- Both are **Solitary Ground-Nesting Bees**, which build separate nests instead of colonies, and play an important role in the **pollination** of wild plants and agricultural crops.
- Worldwide only **13 species of Elaphropoda** and **55 species of Habropoda** are known.
- **About Siang Valley:** a beautiful, biodiversity-rich valley of **Arunachal Pradesh**, named after the **Siang River**, which further ahead is called the **Brahmaputra** in Assam. The region is mainly the home of the **Adi tribe**.

question: Who has recently been appointed as the new Prime Minister of Ukraine?
detail:
- Ukraine has appointed **Serhii Koretskyi** as its new **Prime Minister**.
- He has become the country's **third Prime Minister** during the **Russia–Ukraine War**.
- He replaced the former Prime Minister of Ukraine, **Yulia Svyrydenko**.
- President **Volodymyr Zelenskyy** has given him the responsibility of strengthening **Energy Security, Economic Stability, Defence Production** and **International Aid Management**.
- **About Ukraine:** capital **Kyiv**; President **Volodymyr Zelenskyy**.

question: Operation Southern Readiness 26-2 will be conducted by the Indian Navy in collaboration with which organization?
detail:
- **Operation Southern Readiness 26-2** is a **Multinational Maritime Security Training Exercise**, to be conducted by the **Indian Navy** and the **Combined Maritime Forces (CMF)** under **Combined Task Force (CTF)-154**.
- It will be held from **20–23 July 2026** at the **Southern Naval Command, Kochi (Kerala)**.
- Navies of several countries take part in it and carry out exercises related to maritime security.
- Its objective is to enhance **Maritime Security** and promote better **Training and Coordination** among partner navies.
- **Major participating countries:** **Australia, Canada, Japan, Thailand, UAE, Oman, Djibouti, Seychelles, United States and United Kingdom**.
- Its **first edition** was held in **2023 in Seychelles**, in which **INS Sunayna** took part.
- **Key points:** the **1st edition of 2026** was held in **January 2026 (Kenya)**; this is the **first time it is hosted by India (Southern Naval Command)** — **July 2026**.
- **About CMF:** a **Multinational Naval Partnership** with headquarters at **Bahrain (Manama)**. It currently has **38 member countries**, including **India**. Its aim is to maintain maritime security in the **Indian Ocean, Arabian Sea, Red Sea and Gulf region**.
- **CTF-154** is a special Task Force of the CMF.
- **About Southern Naval Command:** the **Training Command** of the Indian Navy; headquarters **Kochi, Kerala**.

question: What is the name of India's first private-sector orbital-class reusable rocket booster recovery mission?
detail:
- **Mission-02** is an important space mission of India's private space company **Agnikul Cosmos**.
- Under Mission-02, Agnikul Cosmos will test India's first **Private-Sector Orbital-Class Reusable Rocket**.
- The **Agnibaan Rocket** will be tested in this mission so that it can be used again in future as a **Reusable Rocket**.
- The mission will attempt to **recover the First-Stage Booster** of the Agnibaan Rocket from the sea and to **repurpose the Upper Stage** in orbit.
- **Objective:** to develop **Reusable Rocket Technology** and reduce the cost of space launches.
- If successful, **Agnikul** will join the select group of companies in the world that have developed **Reusable Orbital Rocket Technology**.
- **About Agnikul Cosmos:** a Chennai-based, **IIT Madras incubated** Indian **Private Space-Tech Startup** that develops affordable and customizable launch vehicles like the **Agnibaan Rocket** for small satellites. Established **2017**; headquarters **Chennai, Tamil Nadu**.

question: NITI Aayog has proposed a BioEconomy Growth Fund of how much to make India a global BioEconomy powerhouse by 2035?
detail:
- **NITI Aayog** released the report **'Roadmap for Building India as a Leading BioEconomy Powerhouse by 2035'**.
- It proposes a **BioEconomy Growth Fund of ₹50,000 crore** to place India among the world's **Top-3 Biotechnology Powers** by **2035**.
- Its aim is to boost India's BioEconomy and make **Biotechnology and Biomanufacturing** globally competitive.
- **What is BioEconomy:** the economy in which **Biotechnology** is used to develop medicines, agriculture, food products, environmental and industrial products.
- **The ₹50,000 crore BioEconomy Growth Fund:** duration **2026–2035**; it will be used to give financial support to **Biotechnology Startups, Research and Industry**.
- **Other proposals:** a **PLI Scheme for Biomanufacturing** to give incentives to companies making biotechnology-based products, and a proposal to launch **6 National BioMissions** in different fields of biotechnology.
- **Targets:** **2025 — USD 195.3 Billion**; **2035 target — USD 691 Billion**; **2047 target — USD 2.6 Trillion BioEconomy**.

question: According to the FICCI–Deloitte report, India's Processed Food Market is projected to reach approximately how many billion US dollars by 2030?
detail:
- According to the **FICCI–Deloitte** report, India's **Food Processing Sector** can reach the level of about **600 Billion US Dollars by 2030**.
- **FICCI FoodWorld India 2026** — the **17th edition** — was organised in **New Delhi**.
- At this summit **FICCI** and **Deloitte India** released the report **"STEP UP – Scalable Transformation for Efficiency and Profitability to Unleash Progress"**.
- **Technology, AI, Value Addition, Digital Adoption** and **Export-led Growth** will play an important role in this growth.
- **What is the Food Processing Sector:** the industry in which **Raw Agricultural Products** such as wheat, milk, fruits, vegetables and meat are processed to make biscuits, juice, chips, paneer, pickles, packaged food and so on.
- **Key Points:** at present India processes only **12–13%** of its total food output, so there is big potential for **Value Addition**. The **Health & Functional Food** category is growing at **15–20% per year**, roughly double the pace of the general food market.
- **About FICCI:** the **Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry**, India's premier Industry & Business Association, which coordinates between the government and industry on issues of industry, trade and economic development. Established **1927**; headquarters **New Delhi**.
